title: parinari obtusifolia
intro: Parinari obtusifolia, also known as the 'Benguet plum' or 'Katmon', is a tree species native to the Philippines. The bark and leaves of Parinari obtusifolia have been traditionally used in folk medicine due to their medicinal properties, which include antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Parinari obtusifolia are attributed to its ability to reduce fever, alleviate diarrhea, and treat various respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of Parinari obtusifolia can be made by infusing its leaves and bark in hot water to create a tea, or by combining it with other herbs to create a medicinal syrup.
